name: Remote Sensing Crop & Land Mapping
description: This domain covers machine learning approaches for mapping crops, agricultural fields, and land cover from satellite, drone, and street view imagery, as seen in works like 'OpenMapFlow', 'Fields of The World', and 'Semantic Segmentation of Crop Type in Africa'. It spans crop type classification, field boundary segmentation, yield mapping, and specialized detection tasks such as harvest piles, opium poppy cultivation, and irrigation type. Techniques include transformers, LSTMs, attention networks, transfer learning, and data augmentation applied to Landsat and Sentinel data.

background_concepts.why_needed: Several crop-type and land-cover mapping papers frame the task as semantic segmentation but assume the reader knows what pixel-wise labeling and metrics like mIoU mean.

suggested_resources.title: Semantic Segmentation of Remote Sensing Images: Definition, Methods, Datasets and Applications

background_concepts.why_needed: Mapping pipelines take inputs like Sentinel-2 spectral bands and derived indices as given, without explaining what the bands are or their spatial resolutions.

suggested_resources.title: Sentinel-2 Multispectral Imager | NASA Earthdata
Sentinel 2 Bands and Combinations - GIS Geography

background_concepts.why_needed: Feature engineering in crop mapping frequently relies on NDVI and related indices as inputs, treated as common knowledge.

suggested_resources.title: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) | NASA Earthdata
What is NDVI? - GIS Geography

name: Climate-Agriculture Forecasting & Impact Modeling
description: This domain examines how climate change and weather affect agricultural systems through forecasting and impact modeling, including works on precipitation forecasting, drought classification, vegetation greenness prediction, and crop yield loss. Readings like 'Robustly modeling the nonlinear impact of climate change on agriculture' and 'Interpretable machine learning approach to understand U.S. prevented planting events' combine econometrics, physics-informed neural networks, and self-supervised learning. It also addresses adaptation tracking, biological threats like the Quelea bird and wheat streak mosaic virus, and neural weather forecasting.

background_concepts.why_needed: Crop-yield-loss forecasting and weather models invoke PINNs, assuming the reader understands how differential-equation residuals become a training loss term.

suggested_resources.title: A Hands-on Introduction to Physics-Informed Neural Networks (nanoHUB)
Physics-Informed Neural Networks tutorial (PyTorch)

background_concepts.why_needed: The paper combining econometrics and ML assumes familiarity with panel-data / nonlinear temperature-response regressions used to estimate climate impacts on yields.

suggested_resources.title: Differences in Differences (UC Davis lecture notes)

background_concepts.why_needed: Forecasting papers reference S2S lead times and drought indices without defining the forecasting horizons or how they relate to agricultural risk.

suggested_resources.title: NDVI and drought conditions - Climate Data Guide (UCAR)

name: Digital Agricultural Extension & Farmer Information Systems
description: This domain focuses on ICT-based tools for delivering agricultural knowledge and advice to farmers, including participatory video platforms like 'Digital Green' and interactive voice forums like 'Avaaj Otalo'. Readings evaluate the cost-effectiveness of digital discussion tools, causal inference for recommendations, and the challenges of scaling ICT in smallholder agriculture. It also examines when technology fails to fit farmers' actual information-sharing practices, as in rural China.

background_concepts.concept: Causal inference / difference-in-differences for impact evaluation
background_concepts.why_needed: The paper evaluating digital-agriculture recommendations uses causal inference designs to assess adoption effects, assuming knowledge of treatment/control comparisons.

suggested_resources.title: Difference in differences — PyMC example gallery
Causal Inference using Difference-in-Differences (Lecture 1)

name: Agricultural Market Structure & Price Information
description: This domain studies agricultural markets, price discovery, and information systems that connect farmers to buyers, including 'Mobile Mandi', crowd-sourced market information in Liberia, and AI-based market intelligence for farmer collectives. Readings analyze the structure and dynamics of India's mandis and rural market towns, alongside price forecasting, anomaly detection, and online price measurement. It also covers policy issues like demonetization's impact on agricultural trade and smallholder market exclusion.

background_concepts.why_needed: Papers on Indian rural markets discuss mandis, licensed intermediaries, and APMC regulation as background institutions without explaining how they structure trade.

suggested_resources.title: Agricultural Produce Market Committee (APMC) Act - BYJU's
Understanding the APMC / Mandi - Rang De

name: Smallholder Farmer Livelihoods & Agrarian Crisis
description: This domain addresses the socioeconomic realities, struggles, and productivity of smallholder farmers, featuring narrative accounts of India's farm crisis such as 'Ramrao' and 'Everybody Loves a Good Drought'. Readings examine farm size and productivity relationships, financial analysis of rice farming in Kenya, and the consequences of Bt cotton in Odisha. It also explores collective social movement strategies among small farmers.

name: Groundwater, Soil & Sustainable Farming Practices
description: This domain covers sustainable resource management in agriculture, particularly groundwater depletion in India and soil health, including soil organic carbon estimation via graph neural networks and climate-related features. Readings promote regenerative agriculture through tools like 'Soil Carbon Copilots' and residue density segmentation for tillage monitoring. It also applies causal machine learning and domain adaptation to sustainable soil management and characterizing porous media.

background_concepts.concept: GRACE satellite gravimetry for groundwater storage
background_concepts.why_needed: Groundwater depletion papers rely on GRACE terrestrial-water-storage measurements, assuming readers know gravity anomalies can quantify water mass changes.

suggested_resources.title: Groundwater | GRACE Tellus - NASA JPL
Water Storage | GRACE-FO Science - NASA

background_concepts.why_needed: SOC-estimation and regenerative-agriculture papers assume familiarity with what soil organic carbon is, how it is measured, and how management sequesters it.

suggested_resources.title: What is soil organic carbon (SOC)? - FAO Global Soil Partnership
Soil-Based Carbon Sequestration - MIT Climate Portal

name: Agricultural Measurement & Statistical Ground-Truthing
description: This domain focuses on accurate measurement and ground-truthing of agricultural variables, including land area measurement ('From Guesstimates to GPStimates') and irrigation detection and method mapping via Sentinel-2 data and datasets like 'IrrMap'. Readings address flowering onset detection in sparse-label contexts and monitoring disruption events through irrigation detection. The emphasis is on generating reliable data foundations for agricultural analysis.

background_concepts.concept: Sentinel-2 30m surface reflectance and revisit cadence
background_concepts.why_needed: The irrigation-detection and IrrMap datasets rely on Sentinel-2 imagery, spatial resolution and revisit frequency as the measurement substrate without introducing the mission's sensing characteristics.

suggested_resources.title: USGS EROS Archive - Sentinel-2 | U.S. Geological Survey

name: In-field IoT and sensing for precision agriculture
description: This domain covers sensor-based and IoT-enabled systems for precision agriculture, including low-cost radar soil moisture sensing and deep learning frameworks like 'SMArtCast' for forecasting soil moisture. Readings address pest and disease management through datasets like 'Paddy Doctor', integrated pest management with causality and explainability, and chatbot-IoT integration for maize disease detection. It also includes grapevine disease prediction using multi-sensor remote sensing and transformer models.

background_concepts.why_needed: Pest-identification and IPM papers presume knowledge of integrated pest management concepts and disease-forecasting from climate variables.

suggested_resources.title: NDVI Explained (crop health monitoring primer)

name: Agricultural supply chain intermediaries and procurement mechanisms
description: This domain examines the role of intermediaries and procurement mechanisms in agricultural supply chains, including incentive-compatible mechanisms for input procurement through farmer collectives. Readings analyze intermediary transactions in Kenya's agricultural supply chain and the political economy of sugar mills in India. The focus is on how transactions, incentives, and connections shape farmer outcomes.

background_concepts.concept: Impact evaluation / causal identification of policy interventions
background_concepts.why_needed: The political-connections study estimates causal effects of institutional arrangements on supply chains, assuming familiarity with quasi-experimental identification.

suggested_resources.title: Causal Inference using Difference-in-Differences (Lecture 1)

name: Human-centered design of low-cost agricultural tools and machines
description: This domain focuses on designing affordable, appropriate agricultural tools and machinery for smallholder contexts, including developing intermediate machines for high-land agriculture. Readings apply human-centered design principles, as in redesigning agricultural hand tools in Western Kenya within an ICTD framework. The emphasis is on matching technology to local needs and constraints.

name: Climate-smart farming practices and carbon/emissions in agriculture
description: This domain examines how advanced computational methods can improve agricultural sustainability, including using deep reinforcement learning and large-scale simulations to optimize soil carbon sequestration and crop management. It also covers the measurement of greenhouse gas emissions through carbon-smart farming practices and participatory sensing, with a specific focus on rice cultivation. Together, these readings connect AI-driven decision-making with the goal of reducing agriculture's carbon footprint.

background_concepts.why_needed: The soil-carbon RL optimization and carbon-smart farming papers assume the reader understands how management practices transfer atmospheric CO2 into soil organic matter and why this is a climate mitigation lever, without teaching the process.

suggested_resources.title: Soil Organic Carbon in Temperate Managed Ecosystems: A Primer (USDA Climate Hubs)
Soil Carbon Sequestration—Fundamentals | Ohioline

background_concepts.concept: Reinforcement learning (agent, state, action, reward, policy)
background_concepts.why_needed: The crop-management and soil-carbon optimization papers frame farm decisions as an RL/MDP problem and assume familiarity with the agent-environment loop and reward maximization.

suggested_resources.title: Part 1: Key Concepts in RL — OpenAI Spinning Up
🤗 Deep Reinforcement Learning Course - Introduction

name: Water and irrigation infrastructure for food/water security
description: This domain focuses on infrastructure interventions that secure reliable water and energy access for farming communities across diverse geographies. Readings assess the productivity impact of farm ponds in Northern India, explore solar charging kiosks supporting agricultural business in Kenya, and describe rainwater harvesting paired with automated off-grid irrigation in Guatemala. The common thread is building resilient systems that support both food and water security for underserved regions.

name: Alternative and diversified livelihood cultivation systems for smallholders
description: This domain explores innovative and diversified cultivation approaches designed to enhance the livelihoods of smallholder and subsistence farmers. It includes AI and IoT-enabled hydroponic grow tents in South Africa, ecosystem-based sustainable aquaculture, modular oyster mushroom cultivation in low-resource settings, and integrated agriculture and clean energy solutions in Sadivayal Village. These readings emphasize accessible, sustainable production methods that expand income opportunities in resource-constrained environments.
